structure.notes: The required Stage 0 sections and locked partial-identification tuple are present, and the assumptions/checklist are mostly usable, but the theorem organization is not review-stable: the actual outer-envelope theorem is placed in the proof section, Conjecture 1 is refuted only as prose in Main theorems, and the four-cell witness lacks a fully specified finite program in the theorem statement.

novelty.notes: The current derivation is below the orchestrated flagship floor. The solid contribution is a routine outer-envelope inclusion plus a one-cell counterexample to the proposed cycle criterion; the negative result does not name a specific published estimator/workflow claim that is refuted, and the claimed open-set witness is not yet positioned as a generic-class obstruction against a named target.

correctness.notes: The one-cell Conjecture 1 counterexample is arithmetically credible, but Theorem thm:witness is not proved from displayed data: the local rank-tube constraints, active LP basis, primal optimizer tables, dual residuals, and global-potential implication are asserted rather than constructed or derived.

review.verbatim_critique: The note has the right partial-identification anchor and most required Stage 0 sections, but it is not yet an acceptable flagship Stage 0 derivation. The strongest checked part is the Conjecture 1 counterexample in the Counterexample section: the response-type enumeration gives the advertised sharp interval [4/5,1], and the lifted relaxation can indeed reach the lower value 2/5 by choosing the marginal-envelope vertex with mass 6/10 on the tail-failing cell. That correctly refutes the proposed iff criterion as stated. Theorem thm:witness is the main problem. The displayed four-cell marginals and the residual arithmetic show only that the proposed certificate would have circulation 0.14 if it came from a valid lifted endpoint dual. The proof never gives the local rank-copula tube inequalities, the marginal-envelope inequalities, the active IV/dose constraints, the primal transport tables, or the normalized dual variables. Consequently the statements in the proof that a witness basis exists, that the endpoint tables are strictly interior, and that the residuals are the lexicographically normalized dual residuals are assumptions, not verified consequences of the displayed law. The key strict-containment step also needs a theorem connecting full-law pastability to residuals being exact differences of one global vertex potential. The deterministic telescoping identity is true once exactness is known, but the note does not prove that any full-data optimizer must produce exact residuals on the active graph. The open-set conclusion has the same issue: Robinson-style active-basis stability can only be applied after the finite-dimensional polyhedral system and active basis are fully specified. As written, there is no finite certificate a reader can check. On novelty, the current verified mathematics is below the flagship floor. A one-cell counterexample to a proposed diagnostic and a routine outer-envelope inclusion are useful, but the negative-result rules require a named published target or a generic-class obstruction. The related-work section lists relevant OT and DTE papers, but it does not identify a specific published estimator, workflow, or theorem that uses the refuted lifted-cellwise shortcut. To revise, Stage 0 should either demote the witness to a conjectural certificate, or provide a complete finite LP certificate: explicit observed law P, explicit M, V, and C constraints, primal endpoint tables, dual variables, active basis, slack margins, and a proved lemma that full-law pastability implies exact residuals on the pasting graph. It should also reposition the negative result against a named target or state plainly that the current derivation is a narrower subfield counterexample rather than a flagship theorem.

The Conjecture 2 theorem is not yet proved because the active rank-tube faces, primal endpoint tables, dual residuals, observed law, and global-potential necessity are asserted rather than constructed from explicit constraints.

perItemFindings.label: Counterexample, lines 363-449
perItemFindings.one_line: The binary response-type calculation correctly shows Gamma(P,0)=0 while the lifted lower endpoint is 2/5 and the sharp full-law lower endpoint is 4/5.

review.verbatim_critique:
The partial-identification anchor is respected: the locked block in Formal setup keeps the tuple (qid, specialization, P, theta, restrictions, bounding functional), and the section titled Estimand-defining functional appropriately defines bounding functionals rather than panel regressions. The required sections are present, the assumptions are labeled, and the Formalization checklist lists the main finite-support objects, programs, graph residuals, and response-type variables. The main structural defect is that the confirmed Conjecture 1 refutation is not stated as a theorem in Main theorems, while the theorem environment is used for Conjecture 2, whose witness is not fully specified. Later stages would be better served by promoting the binary counterexample to a labeled theorem and either fully specifying the Conjecture 2 construction or demoting it to a conjectural witness.

The Conjecture 1 counterexample in the Counterexample section is mathematically convincing. The observed binary table implies the displayed response-type constraints, the event Y(1)-Y(0) <= 0 fails only at type (1,0), and the sharp full-law lower endpoint is at least 1 - 1/10 - 1/10 = 4/5 with the claimed attainment. The lifted relaxation can choose marginal endpoints mu_1(1)=6/10 and mu_0(0)=6/10 and couple 6/10 mass on (1,0), giving the lower endpoint 2/5. Since the one-cell graph has no cycles, Gamma(P,0)=0. This refutes the iff direction of the proposed cycle-frontier theorem and identifies factual compatibility as the missing condition.

The outer-envelope theorem in the Step-by-step proof is also correct but routine: every admissible full-data law projects to conditional cellwise marginals and couplings, so optimizing over the larger lifted feasible set weakly expands the interval. This is useful setup algebra, not a flagship result.

The Conjecture 2 theorem is the main obstacle. The proof at lines 306-310 says that the witness basis contains endpoint tables pi_s^star in the relevant rank-tube faces, with all Frechet nonnegativity inequalities inactive, but the note never displays those tables or the rank-tube inequalities that make them feasible and optimal. Lines 312-326 only verify arithmetic for residuals that are supplied as data; they do not derive those residuals as lexicographically normalized optimal dual residuals of a stated finite program. Lines 334-338 assert that if a lifted optimizer came from one full-data law then the active residuals would be induced by one global potential, but this is not a consequence of Assumption cycle-stability margin as stated. It is the load-bearing pasting lemma and needs either a proof from the primal-dual KKT system of the explicit full-law program or an added named compatibility assumption. Without that lemma, positive circulation does not by itself rule out a full-data law attaining the same objective value.

The open-set claim has the same problem. Active-basis stability can prove openness once an explicit finite LP, active basis, primal optimizer, dual optimizer, and positive slack table are all given. Here the proof uses unnamed local envelope inequalities, unnamed rank-tube faces, and an unspecified witness basis, so the theorem is not independently checkable from the note.

The novelty is also below the enforced flagship target. The note does not prove the proposed iff frontier; it refutes it. The refutation is useful, but the note does not name a specific published estimator, paper claim, or workflow that relies on the false zero-cycle diagnostic. The remaining positive result is a claimed finite-table strict-containment witness, not a new sharp bound, new identification target, generic-class obstruction, or constructive identifiability theorem. To reach the requested flagship floor, the artifact would need a repaired headline: for example, a theorem giving an iff frontier after adding explicit factual-compatibility constraints, or a generic-class obstruction tied to named published lifted-OT workflows, with a fully specified nondegenerate witness and derivation of the residual certificate.

structure.notes: The required Stage 0 sections and locked PartialID tuple are present, but the main theorem organization is unstable: the routine outer-envelope theorem is placed under the proof section rather than the main-theorem section, and the flagship witness is stated without enough mathematical objects to make the displayed center reviewable as an observable law and feasible program.

novelty.notes: The note has a plausible useful contribution, especially the factual-compatibility counterexample to the proposed cycle criterion, but the accepted derivation tier is below the enforced flagship floor. The negative result does not name a specific published estimator or workflow using the refuted shortcut, and the positive strict-containment witness is not yet a rigorously specified generic-class theorem.

correctness.notes: The outer-envelope theorem and the one-cell binary counterexample appear algebraically correct, but Theorem 1 as a confirmation of Conjecture 2 is not proved from specified primitives. It relies on unexhibited rank-tube constraints, primal endpoint tables, dual residuals, and a global-potential implication that is asserted rather than derived.

perItemFindings.label: Formal setup / observable law P
perItemFindings.one_line: For the four-cell witness, the note specifies conditional potential-outcome marginals but does not specify a compatible observable distribution of (Y,D,Z,X), even though P is the declared observable input.

perItemFindings.label: Formalization checklist
perItemFindings.one_line: The checklist is mostly useful, but it includes several proof-critical objects, such as G(P), residuals, active arcs, and active-basis stability, without typed finite data sufficient to reconstruct the witness.

review.verbatim_critique: The note has the right PartialID anchor and most required Stage 0 sections, and the algebra in the one-cell binary counterexample is convincing: the response-type calculation gives the sharp full-law interval [4/5,1], while the lifted relaxation can reach [2/5,1] with Gamma equal to zero. That is a real factual-compatibility obstruction and should be promoted to a clean labelled theorem. The present flagship theorem, however, is not yet a theorem in the displayed finite model. In the Main theorems section and the proof of Theorem witness, the four-cell construction specifies conditional marginals and a residual table, but it does not specify the finite observable law P of (Y,D,Z,X), the actual marginal-envelope inequalities, the IV/dose system V(P), the rank-copula tube constraints, the endpoint primal tables, or the normalized dual vectors. The proof repeatedly says that the witness basis is specified in the conjecture, that the local envelope inequalities are chosen with slack, and that active residuals from a full-data law would be exact differences of one global potential. Those are exactly the mathematical facts that need to be exhibited or proved. The cycle arithmetic 0.06+0.04+0.03+0.01=0.14 is correct, but arithmetic alone does not certify that those numbers arise from a feasible lifted LP with the stated observed law and restrictions, nor that nonzero circulation prevents full-law pasting under the formal definition of Q(P). The open-set argument also depends on active-basis stability and unique normalized duals, but the active basis and LP are not fully written down. For novelty, the derivation is below the enforced flagship target. The negative Conjecture 1 result is a useful counterexample to the proposed diagnostic, but it is currently a self-refutation of the proposal rather than a named published-method refutation, and the positive Conjecture 2 witness is not sufficiently explicit to support a flagship open-set strict-containment claim. A revision should either recast the paper around the factual-compatibility theorem with a computable response-type feasibility correction, or fully specify and certify the four-cell LP, including observable P, constraints, primal and dual witnesses, and the theorem that full-law pasting forces zero circulation.

reason: Case 6b (spirit) + regression pattern (a): boundary stage_0.5_to_0 with same flag — Conjecture-2 witness lacks finite LP certificate and load-bearing pastability lemma is asserted not derived — repeating across all 3 attempts; proposal_promise_gap is kernel_substituted/direction_only/constructive_object_missing; reviewers explicitly point to a recast (factual-compatibility theorem from the validated Conjecture-1 counterexample) rather than a fixable Stage-0 derivation step. The currently solid material (Conjecture-1 refutation + outer-envelope inclusion) does not clear the flagship novelty floor on its own, and the witness side cannot be made verifiable without research-grade scaffolding the proposal didn't supply. Pivot the angle.
proposed_action: Stage -1: draft a new angle recast around the factual-compatibility correction surfaced by the validated Conjecture-1 counterexample (binary one-cell, [4/5,1] sharp vs lifted [2/5,1] with Γ=0, demonstrating that zero-cycle is insufficient and factual-compatibility is the missing diagnostic). Archive the current pid_ot_dte_marginal_v1.tex. New angle should either (i) name a specific published lifted-cellwise OT/DTE workflow whose diagnostic is refuted by the factual-compatibility obstruction (clearing the negative-result novelty floor), or (ii) propose the corrected iff frontier "outer envelope = sharp ↔ factual-compatibility holds" as the flagship, with a constructive feasibility witness that does NOT rely on the unverified pastability-to-single-potential lemma.
